meaning: adj.闅忔剰灏嗛煶绗﹂暱搴﹀鍑忕殑
examples: At the opening his very first note has a sharper impact, and the florid display work which follows is lighter and gayer, while the first long melody is freer, more expressive in its rubato.
In "Blue in Green, " Hiromi embraces the chord structure and tempo used by pianist Bill Evans, inserting a rubato of her own virtuoso flights amidst the cycle.
